name: GPT-5.5 (xHigh)
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer, reasoning-tuned); codename "Spud"; 'xhigh' (maximum) reasoning-effort configuration
note: Highest reasoning-effort sibling of the base 'GPT-5.5' entry ('reserved for the hardest quality-first workloads' per OpenAI's own guidance). Same weights/pricing/context as the default and High siblings; distinguished by reasoning.effort=xhigh.

results.notes: Explicitly attributed to 'xhigh effort' by benchlm.ai. Numerically identical to the frontiermath-tier1-3 result already recorded on the base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry -- see verificationNotes.
Explicitly attributed to 'xhigh effort' by benchlm.ai. Numerically identical to the frontiermath-tier4 result already recorded on the base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry -- see verificationNotes.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Codex harness, XHigh reasoning effort: Pass rate 26.6% / Score 47.9% (rank 10). Matches this catalog entry's own effortLabel ('xHigh'). Score component used per corpus convention.

GDPval-AA v2 (Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1 component), independently measured on the dedicated 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page (URL slug and page title both confirm this is the xhigh-effort config, distinct from the 'GPT-5.5 (high)' page which scores differently on the same eval). Read by exact rank-position matching against AA's live ranked benchmark chart (GPT-5.5 (xhigh) placed between Claude Opus 4.7 (max) and GPT-5.5 (high), both at 50%). Not previously tracked on this catalog entry. [score corrected during round-6 completeness verify: proposed 50 -> 49.7]

Terminal-Bench v2.1 (AA's harness), independently measured on the 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page. Distinct data point from OpenAI's own self-reported 'Terminal-Bench 2.0: 82.7%' figure (different harness version/measurement); this catalog entry currently has no terminal-bench-2 result at all, so this is new, not a duplicate.

Humanity's Last Exam, independently measured on the 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page (AA's own methodology, likely differs from OpenAI's no-tools condition -- OpenAI's own blog cites 41.4%/52.2% depending on source, none of which is on this catalog entry). Not previously tracked here.
GPQA Diamond, independently measured on the 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page -- ranked #1-2 of 28 models tracked (94%, tied with GPT-5.6 Sol (max)). Not previously tracked on this catalog entry.

verificationNotes: Confirmed as a distinct, independently-benchmarked configuration on Artificial Analysis (model page 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)', explicitly labeled 'Reasoning Effort Label: xhigh' on direct fetch; Intelligence Index score 55, rank #7/188, strictly ahead of the 'high' sibling's 53/#12). benchlm.ai's benchmark table for GPT-5.5 explicitly attributes its FrontierMath v2 figures to 'xhigh effort' (Tier 1-3: 51.7%, Tier 4: 35.4%) — these are numerically identical to the FrontierMath scores already stored on the pre-existing base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry, strongly suggesting that entry's headline benchmark numbers are actually xhigh-tier results mislabeled as the plain/default config. Flagging for the ingesting pipeline to consider migrating those two FrontierMath results (and possibly GDPval/BrowseComp/SWE-Bench Pro/Terminal-Bench, which are likely also xhigh headline figures) from the base entry onto this xHigh entry, and to set the base entry's isBestConfig to false since this sibling is now the true best-of-cohort.
